issues.comment: Objects.requireNonNull checks 'grantType' twice but should check 'rawTokenId' in the last validation, causing rawTokenId null values to pass through validation.

substring(3, 5) extracts the wrong token part (should be substring(4, 6) for grant type at positions 4-5) and the condition logic is inverted - returns false when grant types match instead of when they don't match, causing test assertions to fail incorrectly.
Objects.requireNonNull checks 'grantType' but error message says 'Null rawTokenId not allowed', and should be checking 'rawTokenId' parameter instead, causing incorrect null validation.

Condition 'items[0].substring(3, 5).equals(expectedGrantShortcut)' returns false when grant shortcut matches expected value, but should return false when it does NOT match, causing test assertions to fail when they should pass.
